name: Identity Fusion NG
description: Identity Fusion NG is a connector that allows you to manage Identity Fusion accounts by mapping account attributes from multiple sources, defining new attributes and unique identifiers, and matching accounts with existing identities to prevent duplicates.

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Configure how this connector connects to Identity Security Cloud.

Use Identity Security Cloud API URL to specify the base URL of your ISC tenant (for example, https://tenant.api.identitynow.com).

Provide the Personal Access Token ID and Personal Access Token Secret for an account that has the required API permissions (Sources, Identities, Accounts, Workflows/Forms). These values are used to authenticate all connector operations.

items.label: Identity Security Cloud API URL
items.helpKey: Base URL of your Identity Security Cloud tenant (format: https://<tenant>.api.identitynow.com)

items.label: Personal Access Token ID
items.helpKey: Enter the Client ID from your Personal Access Token. The token must have permissions to manage sources, identities, accounts, and forms

items.label: Personal Access Token secret
items.helpKey: Enter the Client Secret from your Personal Access Token. This value is stored securely and should be rotated periodically

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Define which identities are in scope.

Limit processing using an Identity Scope Query (standard ISC search syntax). Toggle Include identities in the scope to establish a baseline for Match.

items.label: Include identities in the scope?
items.helpKey: When enabled, existing identities are included alongside accounts from your configured sources. Turn this on if you want to define attributes from identity data or use identities as a baseline for detecting potential matches

items.label: Identity Scope Query
items.helpKey: Filter which identities are included using a search query. Use * for all identities, or narrow results with filters like attributes.cloudLifecycleState:active or source.name:"Workday"

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Select and configure authoritative account sources.

  • Source type & behavior: Define role (Authoritative, Record, Orphan) and handling for unmatched non-identity accounts (disable, defer).
  • Aggregation & correlation: Control timing (delays, retries), optimized aggregation, and correlation strategy (in-process or reverse).
  • Filtering & limits: Process subsets via API/JMESPath filters and set batch capacities.

items.label: Source name
items.helpKey: Enter the exact name of the source as it appears in Identity Security Cloud (case-sensitive)

items.label: Enabled
items.helpKey: Turn off to temporarily exclude this source from processing without removing it

items.label: Source type
items.helpKey: Choose how accounts from this source are handled. 'Authoritative accounts' creates new Fusion accounts for unmatched entries (default). 'Records' processes accounts for Map & Define but does not create Fusion accounts for unmatched ones. 'Orphan accounts' skips unmatched accounts entirely, with an option to disable them

items.label: Disable non-matching accounts
items.helpKey: Automatically disable orphan accounts that no longer match any identity after aggregation

items.label: Same-aggregation matching
items.helpKey: After identity matching, also compare to other new unmatched accounts from this aggregation. If the only strong match is one of those peers, defer instead of creating another Fusion identity until a later run. Off: skip that check and use normal unmatched handling. Turn on when the same person might appear as multiple accounts in one run.

items.label: Include record accounts in Match
items.helpKey: Enable when record accounts should be matched against Fusion identities. Disable to skip Match; Map & Define and unique attributes still run.

items.label: Accounts API filter
items.helpKey: Server-side ISC account filter sent to the Accounts API (filters parameter). Use this to reduce records returned from ISC. Reference: https://developer.sailpoint.com/docs/api/v2025/list-accounts

items.label: Accounts JMESPath filter
items.helpKey: Client-side JMESPath expression applied after accounts are fetched (page-wise as { accounts: [...] }). The expression must return an array of account objects to keep. Reference: https://jmespath.org/

items.label: Aggregation batch size
items.helpKey: Limit the number of accounts processed per run. Leave empty to process all accounts. Useful when loading large datasets for the first time

items.label: Account aggregation mode
items.helpKey: Choose when to pull the latest accounts from this source. 'Aggregate before processing' refreshes accounts first. 'Delayed aggregation' refreshes after processing completes. 'Do not aggregate' uses existing account data. Note: delimited file sources do not support aggregation

items.label: Aggregation task result retries
items.helpKey: How many times to check whether an aggregation has finished for this source. Increase if this source takes longer to aggregate. Default: 5

items.label: Aggregation task result wait time (seconds)
items.helpKey: Seconds to wait between aggregation status checks for this source. Default: 60

items.label: Aggregation delay (minutes)
items.helpKey: How many minutes to wait after processing completes before refreshing accounts from this source

items.label: Optimized aggregation
items.helpKey: Only process accounts that have changed since the last run, improving performance. Disable this if you use reverse correlation, which requires all accounts to be reprocessed

items.label: Optimized aggregation
items.helpKey: Only process accounts that have changed since the last run, improving performance. Disable this if you use reverse correlation, which requires all accounts to be reprocessed

items.label: Correlation mode
items.helpKey: Choose how uncorrelated source accounts are linked to identities. 'Correlate missing accounts on aggregation' links them automatically during processing. 'Reverse correlation from managed source' creates/uses a dedicated identity attribute and correlation rule so ISC can match them. 'Do not correlate' leaves them unlinked

items.label: Correlation attribute display name
items.helpKey: Friendly display label for the reverse-correlation attribute shown in Identity Security Cloud

items.label: Correlation attribute name
items.helpKey: Internal name for the attribute used to link accounts via reverse correlation. This must be unique and cannot overlap mapped/defined attributes or existing source schema attributes

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Control account maintenance.

  • History limits: Cap the number of audit history messages stored per account.
  • Delete when empty: Remove the Fusion account if all its underlying source accounts are deleted.
  • Skip missing targets: Ignore source accounts missing a mapped native identity attribute.

items.label: Maximum history messages
items.helpKey: Number of processing history entries to keep per account. Older entries are removed automatically. Default: 10

items.label: Delete accounts with no managed accounts left?
items.helpKey: Automatically remove a Fusion account when all of its linked source accounts have been deleted. Useful for cleaning up after users leave the organization

items.label: Skip accounts with missing ID?
items.helpKey: Skip accounts that are missing a unique identity value. These accounts will be logged so you can review them later

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Map and merge source attributes.

Choose a Default attribute merge mode. Then configure per-attribute rules to target specific source fields, rename them on the Fusion account, and optionally override the merge strategy or pin specific sources.

items.label: Default attribute merge from multiple sources
items.helpKey: When multiple sources provide the same attribute, choose how to combine them. 'First found' uses the value from the highest-priority source. 'Keep a list' stores all values. 'Concatenate' joins values into a single text

items.label: New attribute
items.helpKey: Name for this attribute on the Fusion account. This will appear in the account schema

items.label: Existing attributes
items.helpKey: Source attribute names to pull values from. Names must match the source account attributes exactly (case-sensitive)

items.label: Default attribute merge from multiple sources
items.helpKey: Override the default merge strategy for this specific attribute. Choose 'Source name' to always prefer the value from a specific source

items.label: Source name
items.helpKey: Name of the source whose value should be used for this attribute when available

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Define dynamic computed attributes. Apache Velocity; optional per-definition refresh. Post-template options: case, character normalization, spaces, trim, max length.

Context: mapped attributes as $name; $identity; $accounts (ordered by configured sources, then aggregation order; valid mainAccount moves that snapshot to $accounts[0]); snapshots include source attributes plus _name, _source, _sourceId, _nativeIdentity, IIQDisabled — the composite account key is $originAccount for the origin row only, not duplicated on each snapshot; $account (origin: managed match, or identity row with originIdentityId; if origin is Identities and managed accounts exist, $accounts[0] is used); $previous; $sources (each source name → list of snapshots). Order: top to bottom; each result is available to the next.

Helpers: $Math, $Datefns, $Normalize, $AddressParse, $JSON (stringify / parse for JSON text).

items.label: Normal Attribute Definitions
items.helpKey: Define attributes whose values are defined from templates using mapped account data

items.label: Attribute Name
items.helpKey: Name for this defined attribute on the Fusion account

items.label: Apache Velocity expression
items.helpKey: Velocity template that defines the attribute value. You can reference any mapped attribute by name. Managed account snapshots in $accounts / $sources / $account include _id, source (id, name), schema (id = native identity, name = display), and IIQDisabled; identity-backed $account rows use source.name = Identities (no source.id). Example: #set($i=$firstname.substring(0, 1))$i$lastname produces 'jsmith' from 'John Smith'

items.label: Refresh on each aggregation?
items.helpKey: When enabled, the value is recalculated on every run. When disabled, the value is only set once

items.label: Case selection
items.helpKey: Convert the defined value to a specific letter case

items.label: Normalize special characters?
items.helpKey: Replace accented and special characters with their plain equivalents (e.g. e instead of e with acute)

items.label: Remove spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove all spaces from the defined value

items.label: Trim leading and trailing spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove extra spaces at the beginning and end of the defined value

items.label: Maximum length
items.helpKey: Limit the defined value to this many characters. Leave empty for no limit

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Define persistent unique attributes (e.g. identifiers). Values stay until account reset (e.g. disable/re-enable). Runs after normal definitions on refresh — same Velocity context plus outputs from normal definitions that run first; list order applies within unique definitions too.

Helpers: $Normalize (date, phone, name, fullName, ssn, address); $AddressParse (getCityState, getCityStateCode, parse). Bad inputs usually render empty. $Math / $Datefns; $JSON (stringify, parse) — see Normal Attribute Definitions.

Unique variables: $counter — collision mode: empty first try, then padded suffix (see Minimum counter digits), retries capped by Maximum attempts; auto-appended if missing and no $UUID. Use incremental counter: next value from state; first = Counter start value (default 1). $UUID: fresh v4 per attempt when referenced. Existing values are reused unless this run refreshes the account, the account resets, or a unique value is empty (retry).

items.label: Maximum attempts for unique definition
items.helpKey: Maximum number of tries to find a value that is not already taken. Increase if you have a very large number of existing accounts. Default: 20

items.label: Unique Attribute Definitions
items.helpKey: Define attributes that must have a unique value across all accounts, such as usernames or employee IDs

items.label: Attribute Name
items.helpKey: Name for this unique attribute on the Fusion account

items.label: Apache Velocity expression
items.helpKey: Velocity template that defines the unique value. Use $counter for an automatic number suffix (e.g. jsmith1, jsmith2) or $UUID for a random unique ID. Managed snapshots expose source (id, name) and schema (id, name) on $account / $accounts. Example: #set($i=$firstname.substring(0, 1))$i$lastname$counter

items.label: Minimum counter digits ($counter)
items.helpKey: Minimum number of digits for the counter, padded with leading zeros. For example, 3 digits produces 001, 002, etc.

items.label: Use incremental counter?
items.helpKey: Always assign the next number in sequence instead of only adding a number when there is a conflict. Useful for defining sequential IDs like employee numbers

items.label: Counter start value
items.helpKey: The first number the counter will use. For example, set to 1000 to start numbering from 1000

items.label: Case selection
items.helpKey: Convert the defined value to a specific letter case

items.label: Normalize special characters?
items.helpKey: Replace accented and special characters with their plain equivalents (e.g. e instead of e with acute)

items.label: Remove spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove all spaces from the defined value

items.label: Trim leading and trailing spaces?
items.helpKey: Remove extra spaces at the beginning and end of the defined value

items.label: Maximum length
items.helpKey: Limit the defined value to this many characters. Leave empty for no limit

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Configure similarity-based match detection.

Evaluate matches using Fusion attribute matches to calculate a Combined match score.
  • Algorithms: Text, phonetic, and intelligent gap comparisons.
  • Thresholds & weights: Set minimum scores for individual rules and the overall match. Pass thresholds double as blend weights.
  • Strictness: Enforce mandatory rules or optionally assign to the matched identity without manual review when scores are an exact attribute match (every real rule scored 100 with none skipped).

items.label: Minimum combined match score [0-100]
items.helpKey: Minimum weighted combined similarity (0-100) required for a potential match. Mandatory rules must still meet their own minimums.

items.label: Automatically assign on exact match?
items.helpKey: When every configured attribute rule scores 100 and none were skipped (exact attribute match), assign the managed account to that identity without a review form. Merge and correlation behave like a link-to-existing review, but the fusion account receives the Auto status entitlement only (not Authorized). Only enable when you are confident your matching rules produce accurate results.

items.label: Attribute
items.helpKey: The identity attribute to compare when looking for matches (e.g. firstname, lastname, email)

items.label: Matching algorithm
items.helpKey: Algorithm used to compare values. 'Enhanced Name Matcher' excels at strict formatting and accents. 'Jaro-Winkler' is highly tolerant of typographical errors. 'LIG3' balances gaps, missing fragments, and character variations. 'Dice' is forgiving of total word reordering. 'Double Metaphone' captures English phonetic similarity

options.label: Enhanced Name Matcher
options.docLinkLabel: Strict matching that handles accents and normalization

options.label: Jaro-Winkler
options.docLinkLabel: Mechanical string comparison tolerant of typos

options.label: LIG3
options.docLinkLabel: Advanced hybrid handling missing gaps and character variations

options.label: Dice
options.docLinkLabel: N-gram model highly tolerant of word reordering

options.label: Double Metaphone
options.docLinkLabel: Evaluates strings based on their English pronunciation

items.label: Similarity threshold & weight [0-100]
items.helpKey: One setting, two roles: (1) minimum similarity (0-100) the rule must meet to pass, and (2) blend weight in the combined score—higher means stricter and more influence. Use 0 only for minimal weight; it is treated as weight 1 in the blend.

items.label: Mandatory match
items.helpKey: When enabled, this rule must meet its minimum similarity for a potential match. Mandatory rules are always evaluated even when a value is missing (Skip match if missing does not apply), so absent data can fail the rule. Passing mandatory rules contribute to the weighted combined score like other rules.

items.label: Skip match if missing
items.helpKey: When enabled (default), this rule is skipped if either value is missing (null, undefined, or empty after trimming). When disabled, missing values are still compared and count toward the result.

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Configure manual review for potential matches.

Define Form attributes to display to reviewers and set candidate limits. Assign global reviewers to act as fail-safes and optionally email them processing reports after aggregation runs.

items.label: List of Fusion account attributes to include in form
items.helpKey: Account attributes displayed on the review form to help reviewers compare potential matches. For example: name, email, department

items.label: Maximum candidates per review form
items.helpKey: Maximum number of potential identity matches shown on each manual review form. If more identities qualify, only the highest-scoring candidates are included (combined match score, then stable tie-break). Valid range: 1–15. Default: 3

items.label: Manual review expiration days
items.helpKey: Number of days a reviewer has to respond before the review form expires. Default: 7

items.label: Owners are global reviewers?
items.helpKey: Always add the source owner and any member of the source's assigned governance group as reviewers on every review form, ensuring someone is always assigned to review potential matches

items.label: Send report to owner on aggregation?
items.helpKey: Send a summary report to the source owner after each run, including potential matches, processing outcomes, and source-type breakdown metrics

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Advanced options for troubleshooting and performance.

  • Reset & refresh: Rebuild all Fusion accounts from scratch or force recalculate all Normal-type attributes on every run.
  • Batch limits: Optimize memory usage vs throughput during match detection.
  • Misc: Prevent multiple runs overlapping or stream logs to monitoring services.

items.label: Reset accounts?
items.helpKey: Rebuild all Fusion accounts from scratch on the next run. Use with caution in production and remember to turn this off after it completes

items.label: Managed accounts batch size
items.helpKey: Number of uncorrelated managed accounts processed in each match detection batch. Lower values reduce peak memory usage and may improve stability in constrained environments; higher values can improve throughput at the cost of more memory. Typical tuning range: 25-100. Default: 50

items.label: Force Normal-type attribute refresh on each aggregation?
items.helpKey: Recalculate all computed Normal-type attributes on every run, even if no changes were detected. This may increase processing time but ensures values stay up to date

items.label: Enable concurrency check?
items.helpKey: Prevent multiple aggregations from running at the same time, which could cause conflicts. Recommended to keep enabled

items.label: Enable external logging?
items.helpKey: Send connector logs to an external service (e.g. Splunk) for centralized monitoring and troubleshooting

items.label: External logging URL
items.helpKey: The URL where logs should be sent (e.g. your Splunk or logging service endpoint)

items.label: External logging level
items.helpKey: Choose the detail level for external logs. 'Error' sends only critical issues. 'Debug' sends everything, which is useful for troubleshooting but produces more data

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Fine-tune API and queue behavior.

  • Provisioning timeout: Max wait for operations (default: 300s).
  • Queue: Enable rate limiting and concurrency control.
  • Retry: Auto-retry failed requests (recommended).
  • Processing wait time: Keep-alive interval for long operations.
  • Batching: Group requests for efficiency.

items.label: Provisioning timeout (seconds)
items.helpKey: Maximum time (in seconds) to wait for a provisioning operation to complete. Increase this if you experience timeouts with large batches. Default: 300

items.label: Enable queue?
items.helpKey: Manage the flow of API requests to avoid overloading the server. Controls how many requests run at once and how fast they are sent

items.label: Maximum concurrent requests
items.helpKey: How many requests can run at the same time. Lower values are safer for rate-limited APIs. Default: 8

items.label: Requests per second
items.helpKey: Maximum number of requests sent per second. Reduce this if you are getting rate-limit errors. Default: 8

items.label: Enable retry?
items.helpKey: Automatically retry requests that fail due to temporary errors (e.g. timeouts or rate limits). Recommended for production use

items.label: API request retries
items.helpKey: How many times to retry a failed request before giving up. Default: 20

items.label: Processing wait time (seconds)
items.helpKey: How often (in seconds) to send a progress signal during long operations to prevent timeouts. Default: 60

items.label: Retry delay (milliseconds)
items.helpKey: Time to wait (in milliseconds) before retrying a failed request. The server may override this with its own recommended wait time. Default: 1000

items.label: Batch size
items.helpKey: Number of requests to group in each batch. Default: 250

items.label: Enable priority processing?
items.helpKey: Process critical operations (like saving data) before less urgent ones (like logging). Recommended for best performance

items.sectionHelpMessage:
Delegate processing to external server.

Run connector logic on your own infrastructure.
  • Proxy URL: External endpoint.
  • Proxy password: Secret for authentication (must match PROXY_PASSWORD env var on proxy).

items.label: Enable proxy mode?
items.helpKey: Route all processing through your own server instead of running it in the cloud. Use this if you need to run the connector on your own infrastructure

items.label: Proxy URL
items.helpKey: The URL of your proxy server that will handle connector operations

items.label: Proxy password
items.helpKey: A shared secret used to authenticate with your proxy server. This must match the password configured on the proxy

attributes.name: accounts
attributes.description: Managed account keys (sourceId::nativeIdentity; legacy raw IDs supported)

attributes.name: missing-accounts
attributes.description: Missing managed account keys (sourceId::nativeIdentity; legacy raw IDs supported)

attributes.name: mainAccount
attributes.description: Managed account ID evaluated first when present

attributes.name: originSource
attributes.description: Origin source name (set on creation, immutable)

attributes.name: originAccount
attributes.description: Origin identity ID or managed account key (sourceId::nativeIdentity; legacy raw IDs supported)
